extraction.one_line_summary: HINDLEY Jai won Blockhouse sprint from 6-man group after YATES Simon abandoned with knee injury, LOPEZ Juan Pedro miraculously retained maglia rosa by 12 seconds
tactical_patterns: INEOS (CARAPAZ/PORT) paced entire final climb instead of attacking, allowing 6-man sprint finish reducing time gains.
YATES Simon's knee injury from Etna crash revealed itself only at 12km mark, not earlier climbs.
CARAPAZ lost positioning on final corner allowing HINDLEY to win from front, tactical error in stage-deciding moment.
LOPEZ Juan Pedro lost contact after touch-of-wheels incident mid-climb but limited losses to 1:46, defying GC expectations.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: knee injury from previous Etna crash eliminated him 12km from finish on first serious GC test
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: neutral: INEOS chose steady pacing over attacking despite having PORT as domestique, questionable tactics for stage win
rider_signals.LOPEZ Juan Pedro: positive: survived contact incident and 1:46 loss to retain maglia rosa by 12 seconds, massive mental strength
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: won positioning battle in final corner to take first win since 2020 Giro, superior sprint from reduced group
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: negative: mechanical at critical moment eliminated serious GC contender before final climb even started
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: positive: finished with lead group despite age, matched younger climbers on Blockhouse's 13km at 9-14% gradient
